title: SPX Technologies Completes Acquisition of Crawford United
author: Globe Newswire
description: SPX Technologies has completed its $300 million acquisition of Crawford United Corporation. The deal includes Crawford United's Commercial Air-Handling Equipment segment (Air Enterprises and Rahn Industries), which will expand SPX's HVAC portfolio. Crawford United's Industrial & Transportation Products segment is classified as non-core and will be held for sale as discontinued operations.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The acquisition expands SPX's HVAC portfolio with complementary technologies and manufacturing capabilities, aligns with its capital deployment strategy of acquiring high-engineering content businesses, and is expected to drive long-term value for customers and shareholders.

While shareholders approved the transaction and will receive $83.86 per share in cash, the company ceases to exist as an independent entity. The sentiment is neutral as this represents a completed transaction with predetermined terms rather than a positive or negative development for ongoing operations.

title: SPX Technologies to Report Fourth Quarter and Full-Year 2025 Financial Results and Present 2026 Financial Guidance
author: Na
description: SPX Technologies announced it will release its fourth quarter and full-year 2025 financial results on February 24, 2026, followed by a conference call with CEO Gene Lowe and CFO Mark Carano to discuss financial results and 2026 business outlook. The company also announced the appointment of Daniel Whitman as Vice President, General Counsel & Secretary and is acquiring Crawford United Corporation.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company is proactively communicating financial results and providing forward guidance for 2026, demonstrating transparency and investor engagement. Additionally, the appointment of a new General Counsel and the acquisition of Crawford United suggest active business expansion and organizational strengthening.

Being acquired by a larger, publicly-traded company (SPX Technologies) typically represents a positive development for the acquired company, indicating validation of its business and potential for growth under new ownership.

title: John Kaplan Interviewed on Coffee With Q – SPX Zero DTE Trading Insights With the Q Algo
author: Qamar Zeman
description: Entrepreneur John Kaplan shared his trading experience using the Q Algo, a custom tool for trading SPX zero DTE options, highlighting his successful initial trades and transition from entrepreneurship to day trading.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Article describes successful trading strategy and potential trading opportunities with SPX zero DTE options

title: Q FACTOR Announces Pre-Launch of AI-Powered SaaS Trading Software for ZERO DTE SPX & ES MINI Scalps
author: Qamar Zaman
description: Q FACTOR, a machine-learning SaaS platform, is pre-launching a trading software designed to help traders analyze and execute high-probability setups in 0 Days to Expiration (0DTE) SPX options and ES MINI scalps, focusing on data-driven tactical execution.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mentioned as a trading instrument with no specific positive or negative market commentary

title: SPX Announces Pricing of Public Offering of Common Stock
author: Spx Technologies, Inc.
description: SPX Technologies plans to raise $500 million through a public offering of 2,659,575 common stock shares at $188.00 per share, with major investment banks like BofA Securities, J.P. Morgan, and Wells Fargo Securities managing the offering.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company is conducting a standard public stock offering to raise capital, which is a typical financial strategy for corporate growth and does not inherently signal positive or negative performance
